message:
feat(search,analytics): enrich index lock-file protocol with structured job metadata, harden analytics SQL against schema drift, and add search-asset simulation harness

Three parallel improvements:

**Lock-file / maintenance-state enrichment (asset_state.rs, indexer/mod.rs, lib.rs):**
New SearchMaintenanceJobKind enum (LexicalRefresh / SemanticAcquire) with
lock-value round-trip. SearchMaintenanceSnapshot gains job_id, job_kind,
phase, updated_at_ms, and an orphaned flag (metadata present but lock not
held). IndexRunLockGuard writes all fields on every write_metadata call
and clears the file on Drop. ActiveIndexRunDetails and state_meta_json
surface the new fields in JSON output and CLI display. activity_at_ms in
lexical_state_from_observations prefers updated_at_ms over started_at_ms.

**Analytics SQL hardening (query.rs — bulk of changes):**
canonical_message_metrics_from_sql builds a GROUP BY message_id CTE that
MAX-aggregates only columns that actually exist, preventing query failures
on older schemas. normalized_epoch_millis_sql emits a CASE expression
auto-promoting second-granularity timestamps to milliseconds, fixing
time-filter queries against mixed-epoch databases.
message_metrics_time_sql constructs a COALESCE over whichever timestamp
columns exist across messages, message_metrics, and conversations.
Time-filter WHERE clauses now use the normalization wrapper.

**Search-asset simulation harness (tests/):**
Deterministic simulation harness with BTreeMap-backed fake filesystem,
logical clock, failpoint injection (FailpointId, FailpointEffect), and
load-script stages with contention scripting between ForegroundSearch,
SemanticAcquire, BackgroundSemantic, and LexicalRepair actors.
Integration test driver exercises multi-actor publish-crash recovery
and checksum-mismatch failpoint scenarios.

**Minor cleanup:**
- Manual impl Default replaced by derive(Default) + #[default] on two
  enums (policy.rs, sources/config.rs)
- Nested if-let chains collapsed to let-chains (Rust 2024)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
